title: Great headphones for average use, wires seem very sturdy & never get tangled
text:
These are great headphones for music and movies for the price. I have never owned a big-money pair of headphones & after buying cheap ones that I had to replace multiple times due to breakage, I decided to get these after a recommendation from a friend. After buying them for myself, I also bought them for each of my kids. They're great for average use... i've never been bothered by outside noise, they are comfortable, and the sound is of good quality. They also are much sturdier than the cheapie $10-$20 ones I've gotten before. (My kids haven't broken them, which is a minor miracle)

I do recommend getting a bag or carrying case for them (I use a Crown Royal bag:) it certainly helps protect them and they do seem vulnerable at the hinges where they collapse for storage.

The only thing my kid complains about is the wire is kind of short. I prefer it because the wire is nice and thick and never gets tangled. with my previous cheap ones, it was always the wires t

title: SERVICEABLE HEADPHONES FOR THE PRICE
text: I've had these headphones for 2 years now up to the date of this review. Inexpensive and adequate but obviously not for the audiophile! Light so they don't hurt your ears after long wear. Dynamic range is within it's price range. A reasonable value for a knock around set of headphones. The vinyl coating over the ear covers has worn off on both sides and the covering flakes off onto you when you wear them. I guess it might have been affected by how I must put them on and off. Still using them and they still provide adequate sound.

title: Terrific Budget Headphones
text:
I got these for $7.99 on a lightning deal. They are excellent for the price. I ordered two pair: one for my 3 year old and one as a travel pair of headphones. The sound quality is impressive for the price. These are on the ear headphones, not over the ear. So, passive noise isolation won't be as good. However, they are quite comfortable. I could easily wear these for a couple hours while watching a movie. The physical size of the headphones make them a good choice for children. Sure, they aren't brightly colored, but I'm willing to bet the sound quality and price are much better than you'd pay for a branded set of crappy headphones. Also, I was surprised at the quality of the cord. I was expecting a thin, cheap cord. It's actually fairly thick and tangle resistant. Only complaint I have -- I wish the cord connected at a single point, rather than running to each ear piece. But, again, for the price they're darn good quality.

Update: I've now used these headphones several time

title: Not meant for big heads
text:
Purchased a set of these a couple years back and used them on and off since then. Sound quality is pretty good, with decent bass. If I want to, I can crank the volume up to '11' on my phone without having the audio quality suffer as a result.

That said, these headphones are by no means noise canceling, or even noise muffeling. I'm currently using them in an office environment, and I can clearly hear conversations being held a couple doors down.
Biggest issue I have with these and the main reason I've finally decided to replace them is their size. While I am able to put them on, they begin to hurt from the constant pressure they apply over time. I do not have an abnormally large head, bit these things become uncomfortable after a while. I do not have this issue with other headphones I own. Only reason these have lasted as long as they have, is because I really wasn't using them for any extended period of time, until a couple weeks ago. The previous set of office headphon

title: Lots of bang for not much buck!!
text: These headphones are great!! I wanted a pair of "headphones" that were not "earbuds", and I did lots of looking before I purchased. These had really good ratings, and weren't over priced, so I took a chance on them. I must say I'm glad I did. These are stereo headphones, so they truly sound like a stereo in your ears. They are "on the ear" style, but actually fit more like "over the ear" on me. (I'm a mid-30's female.) The bass is really good, clear sound, comfortable, adjustable, and they're not ugly. Ha! The only thing I wish they had was a cord that could be removed. But I can totally live with that.

title: I wasn't expecting them to be this good!
text: I bought these because my friend recommended me to, and she has great taste! I wanted to buy them in pink but the black pair was cheapest and matched my laptop best, and I'm glad I bought the black pair now. They're very modern-looking and go with everything. The sound quality is incredible and the noise cancellation is shockingly good considering the price. I keep getting yelled at by my mom because I can never hear her when I have these on. It's great, because I don't really want to hear her most of the time LOL. I will definitely repurchase these when the time comes.

title: weak plug
text: kids have been through two pair (each),price is great for the quality but the wiring to the plug is weak.  i know they pull on it and drop them but it's sad that i have to throw away over just the plug wire. replacement plugs are expensive (compared to the cost of the phones) and require solder experience

title: These headphones are light and comfortable with good sound quality
text:
This is exactly what I've been looking for. These headphones are light and comfortable with good sound quality. I can wear them for hours with no discomfort at all. They don't squeeze my head, make my ears sweat or do any of those unpleasant things other headphones have done. My only complaint is that people can't hear me very well through the microphone. I find I have to quickly pull the headphones out if someone calls me while I'm listening.

FYI The cable is about 3 feet long.

But as I bought these primary to listen to music I'm only taking one star off.

title: TORTURE FOR YOUR EARS
text: OH THE PAIN! These headphones have cheap thin padding. After a short time you will feel the hard plastic speakers jabbing you in the ears. But you will have already opened the package and used the headphones. So it will be too bad for you. If you love the sensation of sharp plastic piercing you earlobes, this is the headset for you!

title: Not worth the money, time or effort
text: Luckily I bought two of these, because the first pair stopped working after less than a week after getting them. Don't waste your money.

title: I'd purchase again
text: These are comfy headphones, and the sound is smooth and pleasant.

title: Very satisfied customer here!!
text: Awesome sound from a headphone in this price range I recommend these headphones for the sound qualiy and you won't be sorry

title: Five Stars
text: Nice for the price and good sound to them

title: Breaks Easily
text: These headphones were a Christmas gift for my husband.  Within two weeks of limited use the metal tip separated from the cord and remained embedded in his iPad. While I've been known to pull the cord instead of the tip, he never does this.  The breakage was not due to user error.  I expected better from Sony.

title: OK, but not the best.
text: When it comes to headphones lately that you purchase online, I am seldom ever finding any that work like these two. I have old headphones from 25 years ago that still work flawlessly yet I constantly am going through headphones at least once a year, mostly due to the poor quality of plastic that used. Unfortunately I see the same type of quality in these headphones. Why there adequate in nature they are not at a high-end or even medium and in quality. These are more something that he would find it your discount store but they do work. I did not return them because I needed them simply for a monitoring on my podcast. But they were not used full-time so for just now it works okay and the price mark was reasonable. But if you're wanting something with high-end sound that is made very sturdy this is not that

title: Best for the price point.
text:
It would be an embellishment to say these are the best headphones on Amazon, but I feel it is accurate to say these are the best for the price point of $10-$20. I've purchased about ten different headphones for kids' tablets and all of them have turned out to be junk except these. Search headphones on Amazon and the first items that appear will be 'sponsored' items that are cheapo headphones that will break in short order. Some of them will snap and others will just stop working all together, but the common denominator is they will break on you.

These Sony headphones have great sound quality and plug in easily to a Kindle Fire and also a car auxiliary 3.55 mm audio jack. I thought my kids was just ignoring me when she had these on but then I tried them and you really cannot hear anything around you. I like how the cords are on both sides then meet in the middle, and they don't get tangled the say way other headphones do. I also like how they fold flat, unlike how other

title: Gorgeous and great sound
text: Love the color, comfort and sounds fantastic

title: Worth the price
text: Honestly they worth the money. I paid those headphone 7.99$ but they sound great. Of course they do not match a 100+ dollar headphone but they are not crap. It is very good. I recommend it

title: Sony MDRZX110 ZX Series Stereo Headphones.
text: these are wonderfully comfortable for a person like myself who watches tv all night next to a normal sleeping person. I really think they are GREAT!

title: SONY?????
text: Don't think this is a Sony product .Not good at all. Volume on my devices very low .Actually  bought these  because of microphone to talk on phone. They are not even good for that ,poor volume ,static ,echo ,you  name it. Oh yes...very uncomfortable on ears. Already sent back now awaiting refund.

title: stress on ears
text: puts a lot of pressure on ears, sound is good though

title: Inexpensive sound nice
text: I needed an inexpensive, clean sounding set of head phones for watching video. These score on both accounts.

title: Great deal
text:
While I am a vine voice, I bought these before I became a Viner.

What can you say Sony headphones for $10. (Their price when I got them was 10 bucks l) Great deal considering, I have seen these at Tarjay for $40 regular price.
Noise cancellation-isn’t that good I can still hear things outside of headphones but less, it’s definitely muffled.
Sound quality is good. I have bought Sony headphones in the past and they lasted a really long time. I expect these will last just as long.

title: They were comfortable, however and we liked that they folded
text: The cable was shorting out - it only worked if you held the cable still at the insertion point in the device you were using. We are returning them. They were comfortable, however and we liked that they folded.

title: Poor quality.
text: I bought these on May 15 on July 2 the right side stopped working.

title: gift item
text: it is a gift so I don't know but it is what my nephew wanted

title: They are well worth the price.
text: I thought they were pretty nice for the price, they could have a longer cord  but that is easily taken care of.

title: Thanks Sony!
text:
Sound quality 👌
Sony is the best!

title: Great quality sound,  softer,  $11, yup
text:
I was surprised at the sound quality from these phones right out of the box. I'm a fan of the Sony sound and normally use MDR-7506 with my home equipment. These have balanced highs and lows, and a good stereo field. The only caveat they don't go really loud. They're adequate, probably as loud as my old ears should listen anyway. But i turn my phone up all the way which is unnecessary with my earbuds. For $11, definite buy.

Update: I got my 3.5mm to 1/4" adapters today and was able to try these with my home theater stereo in place of my 7506's. Looks like I already mentioned how good they sound. They weren't very loud on my Amazon tablet. They were better with a handheld radio. Softer on my phone. With my home stereo (Sony) they were amply loud and surprisingly good. I don't like "on-ear" phones. But the reproduction of this pair was excellent and lives up to the Sony standard, for the most part. Obviously you have to have a device that pushes enough watts to p

title: Good products for Kids
text: No noise cancellation but not bad, it was a gift to my little cousin.  He still enjoys it and feels like he is with the big kids.  Noise quality is good and doesn't go too high which is fine, again I gave it to a 10yr child. Great product.

title: Needed quick quality with little fuss and got a lot more for my money.
text:
I certainly didn't expect anything of audiophile quality or anything astounding. Knowing how much I'm spending is the context for the review.

It's a good pair of headphones.

They're lightweight, comfortable, the earpads stay reasonably cool, the cord is just long enough to listen to at a desk (and that's a good thing), and they sound awfully good for what I paid. They also look pretty okay. The fact that they fold flat is a nice little bonus but for a pair of utility headphones, I think these are a home run.

So as a sound engineer and a musician I would put it this way. They're not high fidelity headphones, nor do they work for performance. They won't work for reference/studio work at all. They're too flimsy to call a solid pair, but for casually listening to music while multi-tasking at home or for watching a streaming video, these do just great. I think there are a number of good headphones like this one you can get for less than $20 or so. Th

title: Five Stars
text: Got a great deal on these and they are good quality...Sony is a good name

title: Returned - Static When Playing
text: The headphones played a static sound whenever it was first turned on and then occasionally while playing songs.  Not at all the quality described by other reviewers.

title: Great sound and comfortable.
text: These headphones are comfortable. I love the over the ear design because they do somewhat block out the other sounds in the room. I'm not an audiophile but I think the sound produced by these headphones is perfect for the discerning listener. The price was better than I could find anywhere else. Around $15. They are lightweight so they are easy on your head and easy to carry around.

title: quality
text: I bought a portable stereo for use when the power goes out, and it did. The cheap headphones broke and I bought these. So far they are great and for the money, you can't beat them.

title: Works good for what needed
text: Hubby likes them he needed them just for light music while writing

title: Good sound, but too flimsy.
text: I really liked the price to sound quality ratio of these headphones HOWEVER the jack is very flimsy. I broke my first pair sitting down with my phone in my pocket. The jack bent, and from then on I had to hold the cord at a particular angle to get sound in both ears. I assumed the fault was mine and bought a second pair, but within a week the cord got caught on a doorknob I was walking past, and with very little force, the jack snapped right in half, leaving the metal part in my phone. I don't think I can afford to keep replacing these every month.

title: Unfortunate design flaw
text: This is my umpteenth pair of Sony headphones. I rely on Sony for sound quality, durability, reliability and value in my business and personal connections.  This pair was an exception, perhaps a design or production  flaw? In less than a month, main wire frazzled and headphones no longer work at all. Major disappointment.  Warranty expired, so total loss. Sony/ Manufacturer, will you replace at no cost?

title: I've caught other brands on sale for the same price and have gotten a lot better sound. Worst part is everyone can her my ...
text: Sound is ok but nothing to write about. I've caught other brands on sale for the same price and have gotten a lot better sound. Worst part is everyone can her my music when I use them so it kind of defeats the purpose. Take a pass on these.

title: Look elsewhere for reliable headphones
text:
My old headset has been around a while, and maybe that's what makes these feel "off." The fit is a bit on the snug side (which seems common with many headsets). The sound also seemed a bit "heavy." Maybe it's just me. After lowering the volume control a little, the sound quality improved, so perhaps I just grew accustomed to the old headset I've been using. Overall, for the price, the quality is very good. Headset cord could have been longer. I use these for gaming, so I really need to watch if I want to get up to reach for something as I don't have several feet of excess cord to work with.

* UPDATE *

Had to drop my rating to two stars. Nice headphones, but my pair had the left speaker start to fail not even a month after they arrived, and they didn't see much use. I expect better quality from Sony. :-(

title: Decent value for the price.
text:
The ear pieces never sit quite flat on my (large) head. My daughter has the same issue, despite having a smaller head.
The sound is good, but not exceptional. After a month of regular use, I have noticed no problems with reliability.

Decent value for the price.

title: The audio quality is garbage
text: Look nice, lay flat, and comfortable on the ears but none of that really matters when the drivers are so weak that I can barely even hear my videos or music. I wear these in a very quiet room at night and the audio levels are just too weak. I'm not very picky about this stuff and far from being an audio enthusiast. But this is inadequate even for me. I had the Amazon Choice ones for 2 years (about the same price) and they were far and away better than these.

title: Five Stars
text: Perfect for the price. Audio bleeds out a bit but great highs and mids. Decent bass

title: Still Work Over 5 Years Later
text: Bought these in December 2014 - it's June 2020 and they STILL work. Just thought I would write a quick review since that is a pretty good lifespan for a pair of very inexpensive headphones. I do not use them a whole lot, but still....

title: Did not realize how terrible earbuds are until I tried these
text: Bass got massively boosted compared to my earbuds... Sturdier cord (I hope, certainly thicker)... Play lower volume hear it louder... all around improvement from my previous set.

title: Extremely Bulky. Not Travel Friendly!
text: Traveling and needed an additional headphone. Thought we'd give the "Sony MDRZX110/BLK ZX Series Stereo Headphones (Black)" a try. We found it to be extremely bulky and quite heavy. This is contrary to the product description. Thus, not travel friendly. It is too big for younger kids and uncomfortable for adults to wear as well. The easy to fold swivel design is a plus but definitely not good enough to recommend this headphone. Furthermore, the padding around the headphones appears to be of poor quality. Hence, durability is questionable. Expensive for the quality. Expected better.

title: Very fragile.
text:
Sound quality is only mediocre. The band broke just days after receiving them in the mail. It broke as they were being folded.
Already taken by trash collectors.

title: good value
text: good long cord. comfortable wear.

title: It's too bad because the sound is good and it really blocks ...
text: After 10 minutes I can't stand the pain! It's too bad because the sound is good and it really blocks out other noise but they hurt my little ears!

title: These are good enough that I can't imagine why I would need ...
text: I have never had a high dollar pair of headphones.  These are good enough that I can't imagine why I would need a high dollar pair.  I use them with my iPad for movies and occasionally with my phone for music.

title: Great Headphones For The Price
text: These very inexpensive headphones are exactly what I need for walking and traveling.  While they are not as noise canceling as more expensive headphones I've owned, these under $15 Sony headphones are quite the bargain.  The sound is very good and the cord length is neither too long or too short.  For me they're just right.

title: Not the sound clarity I wanted
text: Not the sound clarity I wanted

title: Cord is far too short
text: The sound quality is fine, especially for the price. However, I was dismayed to find that the cord is incredibly short and that I will need to return these headphones if I cannot find an inexpensive extender. Which could introduce static. So count me dissatisfied. I should have just bought another pair of Sennheisers. You probably should too. I've had two pairs of Sennheisers, each lasted several years of almost daily use, and they are an excellent value.

title: Best value for the money yet.
text: Very light and comfortable. Exceeds my expectations, especially considering I got them for $10. I prefer these to the $70 over-the-ear headphones I bought a few years back, since those often leave my ears sore after a few hours. The sound is adequate for my needs. I am a writer and sit and write for long periods of time. I enjoy listening to classical music while I write, and these headphones suit that purpose well. I would purchase these again, and in fact, will likely purchase a pair to take with me when I travel.

title: OK for video camcorder audio monitoring
text: Not high quality for audiophiles but the folding earphones make them easy to carry to monitor my Sony video camcorders.

title: Fine until broken
text:
I bought this 2.5 years ago; for the last few months, the sound has been intermittent, and I lost all sound in the right ear today.

Otherwise, these were fine; I just listed to various stuff while working; the sound seemed fine to me. They were comfortable on the ears. The ear parts have not fallen apart.

But I would have preferred them to have lasted longer than a couple of years.

The cord is a bit short, so I've had to use a extension cable. It's reasonable if you are working at the computer where it's plugged into, but I had these plugged into one computer, and would frequently work on other computers in my office.

I'm not sure if I wanted to give this 3 or 4 stars; I'll settle on 3.4. I'm looking around for another alternative, but I may try again if nothing better shows up.

title: Pretty good headset!
text: For the price this headset can't be beat! I prefer the old Sony MDR-ZX100, but it seems they don't make them anymore.

title: Highly recommend these headphones
text: I am elderly, retired and a bit hard of hearing. Listen to ballgames, videos and music for hours a day; and I listen to everything using these headphones so that I won't disturb my neighbors. They are comfortable and the sound quality is amazing for headphones at this price. Still have a pair of ZX-100s that I've been using for years and they continue to work perfectly; although the material that covers the padding is beginning to flake off onto my ears. That's why I got the ZX-110s which, basically, are the same thing. Highly recommend these headphones.

title: You get what you pay for
text: I should of known better then to buy headphones for this price, but always liked Sony products. For the price they are OK but they feel cheap and sound really like cheap headphones. They are small and feel like they are a kids size. You can get a lot better headphones for not that much more. I will give these to the thrift store, not worth using to me.

title: Felt like my old headphones but new!
text: I bought these because it reminds me of my old headphones but grand new! As I first listening it, it sounds AMAZING! The sound quality is a total head banger and it gave me a mind orgasm every time I put these on. Having these new pair really takes me back. Blast from the past!

title: Great for the price.
text:
I gave it 5 stars because of the price and sound quality. If these cost $50 instead of $15 they would get maybe 2 or 3 stars. But with these I apply the 80/20 rule.....at 20% of the price of higher end headphones you still get 80% of the quality. With the 80/20 rule its the other 20% of quality that can really drive the price up :-)

Build Quality; Good enough, little cheapy/plasticy. The padded ear pieces are good enough, and the chord is a nice bonus as its thicker (less prone to damage and tangeling). One mark against them is I found the padding to be a little too much, making my ears a little warm after a couple hours. Some headphones have less padding and a more open design.

Sound; With brands like Bose and Beats I expect a bias in terms of sound profile (eg. too much hi/lo, too much bass, etc...). With the Sony's, the profile is pretty even from the low to mids to highs. Songs with heavier bass sounded fine, with the bass neither under or over emphasized. In

title: READ THIS!
text: OK for a computer but don't waste your dough for music. If you want a GREAT inexpensive set for music that gives you sounds you never heard, buy a set of SONY RP-HTF600 SONY headphones for $26.  They are OUTSTANDING and better than the $300 with a guys name on them brand!

title: Don't bother
text: Got this based on a "best headphones" list...should have saved my money. Audio is leaky, headphones aren't comfortable, and have that flimsy, plasticky feeling. The folding option doesn't even get them folded down to be much smaller, so it's kind of useless. The only nice piece is the in-line mike for use with my home office. Otherwise, I'd pass.

title: Would not recommend
text: One side stopped working just a few months after purchase

title: prety good.
text: prety good. but i have to say they arent any better than regular headphones. they only lasted about a mounth and a half for me. but what can you expect for 10 dollers right?

title: Mediocre
text: Too small and hurts ears if worn too long.  And I have a small hat size head.  For the price it's "OK".

title: Died within a year but fixed it
text: As far as sound quality goes, it is very good. The headset is comfortable and fits both my child and me. Easy to adjust. However, I originally gave it 1 star because one side stopped working within a few months. Today I decided to open it up to see if a wire came loose. Turned out, there was a knot within the ear piece to prevent the wire from being pulled. The knot was very tight. I loosened it up a bit and the headset worked again! I revised my rating to 3 stars and will see if it continues to work. If it does, I'll revise my rating to a higher rating.

title: Toy
text: Well, true enough : whatever you pay for that's what you get. So, don't be fooled by big name (Sony) this fairly small headphones are for kids for playing computer games than for listening to music.  I'm very disappointed, doesn't worth that 20 bucks. I don't like it, I won't use it.

title: Low quality/buyer beware
text: Low quality headphones.  The wires are so thin they cannot handle more than two months of use.  I will not buy this brand again

title: nice, inexpensive headphone.
text: Comfortable, ok bass, not heavy. Not as loud as ear buds. I am happy with the purchase. Button works. works with the Motorola Turbo Phone. Android.

title: Amazing value.
text: Can't beat the sound for less than $18. Clear with just enough bass. Yes, you can spend more money. Yes, you might get better sound. You won't find better sound at this price. They are also solidly built. They flatten easily for storage and are comfortable to wear. I use these for recording. I can hear myself and my music clearly.

title: Great quality
text: These are great quality for the money. Very comfortable and great sound.

title: Worked well... for a couple months
text:
I would have given these 5 stars. However, I have had them just a couple months and they now do not stay plugged in well. The sound cuts in and out when the cord moves. Other headphones work perfectly in the device... so it is not the jack.

Disappointed and still looking for a good set of headphones that will last.

title: Didn't last one week!  Earphone attachment to band made poorly.
text:
I love Sony products and have never had a problem with any until now. The earphone attachment to the headband is made VERY poorly. (See picture for where it disconnected.) There is a "pea" sized ball that inserts into the base of the headband. The earphone will not stay in the headband now.
I don't know if a tiny screw came out or what. The area is black and very small so even with a magnifying glass you can't tell if a screw came out, and, I see no plastic pieces broken or missing. It didn't last a week. This highly used area on these headphones (due to the earphones that fold up and in for storage) should be better made for durability ..... and we were not rough with this set. What a waste of a purchase.

title: Good, affordable product
text: Bought these to use for my electronic piano. Definitely one of the most affordable options I found. Comfortable to wear for long playing times, even when I wear my glasses with them. Good sound quality.

title: Good, affordable product
text: Bought these to use for my electronic piano. Definitely one of the most affordable options I found. Comfortable to wear for long playing times, even when I wear my glasses with them. Good sound quality.

title: I like these even better than Amazon's fancy $20 earbuds, and I gave them 5 stars. These deserve at least six stars.
text:
Sometimes, I use my Amazon Earbuds to listen to music in a restaurant or other places where I should be quiet, but these Stereo Headphones sound even better. I wish they'd bit in my vest pockets easier.

My ears are highly satisfied, and join my fingers in highly recommending them to you.

title: Great!
text: So far so good. For $10 i'm pretty sure this is the best possible headset you can get. Good sound I can hear the highs lows, bass etc. I only take one star because the materials do feel quite cheap when you wear it, but hey its $10 you can't ask for anything more for this price. Sony delivers Amazing headset again. Will buy again if needed. Recommend!

title: $14 headphones, but they don't feel like Sony.
text: They are $14 headphones so I wasn't expecting $100 experiences and they get the job done, but I guess with the name brand Sony, I was expecting a bit more. The cups get hot and sweaty on me, they don't sit firm to my head and I have a BIG head. (I often have one ear cup behind my ear. Sound quality is passable but not what I expect from Sony. However. They are $14... Sooooo am I expecting too much?

title: Not sturdy
text: ... but for $15 I guess I should have expected that. Just wanted headphones without a mic for a reasonable price. Back to the drawing board.

title: fantastic for the price
text: These have excellent frequency range and sou d quality.

title: One Star
text: Broke the first time i put it on my head.

title: Meh
text: They don't fit my phone properly. I have had many Sony products in my life but I was quite disappointed with these.

title: Great headphones for a low price!
text: Purchased as a gift for my 24 year old son.  He is very pleased with these headphones and uses constantly with his smartphone as well as computer.

title: Day 1 blown These are going back get Panasonic for $2 more
text: First day with these headphones Very low sound and very static sound like also very disorted speakes sound blown and they do not get loud at all like $1 earbuds are better for higher sound so dissapointed theses are going back

title: A simple inexpensive set
text: These are a simple inexpensive set for general use... don't expect the greatest sound.  They do not have their own volume control, which I did not need anyway.

title: Great headphones
text: I bought these for my children to use their tablets while staying quiet for appointments for their siblings. They are awesome. My husband loves them so much he’s been taking some from the kids and watching movies in bed while I sleep. I can’t hear a thing. Best headphones ever.

title: Perfect!
text: Great headphones.  Got these at a great price! Perfect for back to school and the sound quality is great!

title: Ok for the cost. Meh at best.
text: Over All these headphones are a good deal for the $. However don't expect anything but mediocre quality with the sound quality which because distorted and then corrects itself. Thus seems to happen if watching a movie with a lot of sound affects. The fit is good. Better than those cheap buds that are constantly falling out of the ear. But again, for the low $ it is an overall good set of headphones. Worth mentioning that the packaging was nothing more than a plastic sack. I thought that odd for a brand like Sony.

title: Not noise canceling, but ok for what they are...
text: These are not advertised as noise canceling, and that’s why they are inexpensive. I wish I had not bought them because turns out noise canceling is 1 million times better.  They're fine for kids playing games.

title: Good value for the buck!
text: These are a good value for the buck. I just need them for playing bingo or listening to music. They are not noise canceling, so if that's what you want. Keep on looking!

title: Great sound, comfortable.
text: These are great. Sound is good. Might pay a little more for ones with the integration microphone next time, but these are perfect for my 11 yr old.

title: Nice but small
text: I didn't like these very much. There small and don't fit very well.

title: Durable, nice for $20. Saved me money.
text:
This is a good pair of inexpensive headphones. They are durable. I have bought both the black and white models - just not for myself. I prefer the white ones since they are easier to find in clutter. These can not touch my SONY V6 headphones, not even close. Of course, I don't want anyone to touch my SONY V6 headphones so these fill that gap nicely. After more than two years of use I was surprised that these did not fall apart. These are not that comfortable but that's not the point, these are nice for being $20. In this same price range I paid more than this for ear bud headphones, all of which fell apart. Most of my ear-bud headphones suffered the same fate of having their cables break on the inside.

it's not fare to compare these to more expensive headphones. If you are buying these for yourself and can afford it, of course get better ones.

title: Very Nice
text: For the price, these are a great value. Happy Customer.

title: Good pair for cheapies.
text: Good sound for this price. I didn’t know what to expect with how reasonable these are.  I really understand now why people prefer noise cancellation though they do work well - especially considering the price difference. The sound is great for cheapies and it has a long cord. I will be repurchasing other pairs.

title: good sound for the money
text: nice, decent sound...use for my tablet..no problems and arrived on time and good condition...reasonable price..will order again.

title: Best Headphones for the Price
text: Purchased for my mom in her 80's enjoying classical and rock music; she couldn't rave enough about yhese earphones with great stereo sound and comfortable ear pads. I highly recommend them and purchased another pair as a gift!

title: Nice headphones, good for smaller heads.
text: These are great, especially if you have a smaller head. :) I have a pair of these both at home and at work, and they're really comfortable. Sound is good.

title: What a deal!
text: Bought it for playback of voice but it is so comfortable and fidelity so good that I prefer it to my $180 Beyerdynamics. Bass is not real loud but its there and nicely balanced. They’re very light, cushions  seal nicely and with only light pressure. The impedence is quite low so they’re not real loud with an iPhone but more than loud enough for my purposes.
